_risk_level_doc: Score persistence architecture change; touches 2 scripts and multiple test files; no auth/security boundary; no public API; self-contained to score subsystem.

requirement.original: Score-update CAS + lifecycle log: replace direct score writes with append-only log entries {ts, agent, event, prev_score, new_score, delta, actor, reason}. Reads use latest entry; writes use Compare-And-Swap on prev_score. User observed score drift `dev 81→73` between events with no event-log explanation.
requirement.clarified: Replace agent-scores.json write path in score-update.sh with append-only JSONL writes to logs/lifecycle.jsonl (remove agent-scores.json write — lifecycle.jsonl is sole source of truth). Add optional CAS validation (--expected-prev-score) that exits 3 on conflict. Switch score-inject.sh to read lifecycle.jsonl. Run baseline-import migration to anchor existing scores. Fully rewrite test_AC4 to target JSONL semantics. Update doc-sync SKIP_FILES. No .gitignore changes needed (logs/lifecycle.jsonl.lock already covered by logs/* at .gitignore:46).
requirement.what: Append-only JSONL score log with optional CAS, baseline migration, and reader migration
requirement.why: Prevent score drift (dev 81→73 undetected) and provide per-event audit trail

score-update.sh does NOT write to agent-scores.json after M1 lands; lifecycle.jsonl is the sole score source of truth
score-inject.sh reads from lifecycle.jsonl (not agent-scores.json) under shared flock
CAS conflict (wrong --expected-prev-score) exits 3 with no JSONL append
lifecycle-baseline-import.sh runs idempotently and anchors all 21 canonical agents

lifecycle.jsonl and lifecycle.jsonl.lock are in SKIP_FILES/SKIP_NAMES in both doc-sync files
lifecycle-baseline-import.sh populates all 21 canonical agents on first run (logs/lifecycle.jsonl has 21 lines each with event=score_baseline_import)

agent-scores.json is migration input only; score-update.sh MUST NOT write to agent-scores.json after M1 lands (no post-migration write path)
Lock file for lifecycle is logs/lifecycle.jsonl.lock (separate from agent-scores.json.lock)
lifecycle.jsonl itself is already tracked; only the .lock file needs gitignore

out_of_scope_observations.observation: agent-scores.json is gitignored at .gitignore:87 (/agent-scores.json). Saying lifecycle.jsonl is tracked 'same as agent-scores.json' is inaccurate — lifecycle.jsonl has an explicit !logs/lifecycle.jsonl exception while agent-scores.json is excluded. No code change needed; factual clarification only.
Caller passes only --agent --event --note. After this cycle, --expected-prev-score is available as optional; no close.md change is required. Should Have S1 documents exit 3 handling.

Codex Issue 7: tests need hermetic lifecycle path isolation. Adding --lifecycle-file CLI param to score-update.sh, score-inject.sh, and lifecycle-baseline-import.sh would allow tests to use temp files without mutating logs/lifecycle.jsonl. Enhancement beyond R9 user-stated scope; M8 requires dev to isolate test from real lifecycle file via dev-chosen mechanism; --lifecycle-file is one valid approach but not mandated by this spec.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: dev score drifted from 81 to 73 with no event-log explanation
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: score-update.sh acquires and RELEASES exclusive flock independently for each of 3-6 sequential calls in a close cycle. An out-of-band concurrent write (another process, another session) between any two sequential calls can produce a stale read on the next call, resulting in score state that diverges from the expected sequential accumulation. No per-event audit trail means the drift cannot be diagnosed post-hoc.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — design limitation present since score-update.sh inception
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Original score-update.sh was designed for single-writer scenarios. Multi-call close cycles were added later without updating the consistency model.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Six independent lock acquisitions in close.md:415-459 create six windows for concurrent interleaving. No audit log means drift is invisible.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Issue existed before cycle 20260522-000000; arch-7 phase 2 was deferred from that cycle as requiring dedicated implementation
observations: logs/lifecycle.jsonl: 0 bytes (observed: architect runtime inspection, confirmed by Codex)
agent-scores.json: 55,909 bytes, 208 history entries across ba/dev/qa (observed: architect runtime inspection)
score-update.sh lines 63-68: exec 9>LOCK_FILE; flock -w 10 9; flock releases on shell exit (inferred from source read)
commands/close.md:415-459: 3 independent score-update calls for close outcome + 3 for user rating (inferred from source read)
test_AC4: asserts uncapped_delta==108 and delta==8; current EVENT_DELTAS gives ba/close_success_qa_pass=+1 so actual values are 101 and 1 (observed: Codex runtime failure AssertionError at line 81)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred for drift mechanism; observed for file sizes, JSONL state, test failure)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Drift mechanism is structural (design of lock-release between calls); cannot be directly reproduced in this CLI environment without introducing concurrent writes. Test_AC4 failure confirmed by Codex runtime execution.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: scripts/score-update.sh:63-68 (lock acquisition/release) and commands/close.md:415-459 (six sequential independent calls)

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: Not a regression (design limitation since inception); bisect not applicable

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: replace direct score writes with append-only log entries {ts, agent, event, prev_score, new_score, delta, actor, reason}
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: score-update.sh appends one JSONL line per invocation to logs/lifecycle.jsonl under exclusive flock, containing all 9 mandatory fields: ts, agent, event, prev_score, new_score, delta, unclamped_score, actor, reason. unclamped_score = prev_score + delta (pre-clamp; may exceed 100 or be below 0). new_score = max(0, min(100, unclamped_score)). The existing --note CLI param populates the 'reason' field. The emitted JSON must contain 'reason' (not 'note') and 'unclamped_score' (not 'uncapped_delta').
score-inject.sh reads logs/lifecycle.jsonl under shared flock and uses the latest new_score for the named agent to compute rank/range
score-update.sh with --expected-prev-score exits 3 (no append) when latest new_score for agent != expected_prev_score

Orchestrator: calls score-inject.sh 3x per cycle (pre-dispatch) and score-update.sh 1-2x (post-QA)

component_chain.role: Writer: acquires exclusive flock, reads agent-scores.json, mutates, writes atomically. Must become: acquires exclusive flock, appends to lifecycle.jsonl.
Reader: reads agent-scores.json, emits rank/range/recent-events injection block. Must become: reads lifecycle.jsonl under shared flock.
Primary score store (currently empty; will receive all new score events)

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if ! flock -w 10 9; then echo "${SCRIPT_NAME}: failed to acquire lock..." >&2; exit 2; fi
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Lock acquisition timeout guard; exits 2 on timeout

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if agent not in CANONICAL_AGENTS: sys.exit(1)
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Unknown agent guard; exits 1 on unknown agent

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if event not in EVENT_DELTAS: sys.exit(1)
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Unknown event guard; exits 1 on unknown event

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if [[ ! -f "${SCORES_FILE}" ]]; then ... neutral mid-tier fallback ... fi
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Missing scores file fallback; emits neutral mid-tier instead of failing

context.codebase_state: lifecycle.jsonl empty (0 bytes, tracked in git); agent-scores.json has 208 history entries across ba/dev/qa; score-update.sh uses agent-scores.json write path; score-inject.sh reads agent-scores.json
recent_commits: af96c61 fix(infra): M1-M4 gitignore exceptions, install mirror, uncapped_delta

development_approach.strategy: Step 1: update score-update.sh — remove agent-scores.json write path; rename emitted field 'note' to 'reason'; add JSONL append under exclusive flock; add --expected-prev-score optional CAS param; add exit 3. Step 2: create lifecycle-baseline-import.sh. Step 3: update score-inject.sh to read lifecycle.jsonl under shared flock. Step 4: update doc-sync SKIP_FILES. Step 5: update test fixtures (M6 malformed branch expectation change; M7 new CAS tests). Step 6: rewrite test_AC4 to target JSONL semantics (M8 — full body rewrite, not just numeric updates). Step 7: run lifecycle-baseline-import.sh once on the real lifecycle.jsonl. NOTE: .gitignore requires no changes (logs/lifecycle.jsonl.lock already covered by logs/* at line 46).
development_approach.validation_approach: Run test-lifecycle-cas.sh (new CAS tests). Run test-inject-branches.sh (updated fixture test). Run test_AC4 (corrected delta). Verify lifecycle.jsonl receives entries. Verify score-inject.sh emits correct rank from lifecycle.jsonl. Verify exit 3 on CAS conflict.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 12 findings. Key issues: schema should use 'reason' not 'note' as primary JSONL field; schema is over-expanded (event_id/seq/prev_event_id/batch are ABA-prevention nice-to-have but not strictly required by user requirement); --expected-prev-event-id is stronger than the CAS-on-prev_score requirement; caller-side CAS is under-specified; derived agent-scores.json cache is expansionist; baseline-import idempotency was wrong (must skip agents with ANY lifecycle entry, not just baseline entries); lifecycle.jsonl.lock must be explicitly gitignored; malformed JSONL must error not fall back neutrally; baseline events must be filtered from recent-events display; CAS test misplaced under score-inject-contract; test_AC4 fix is maintenance not R9 functionality; append-only invariant needs concrete ACs.

findings.issue: Schema uses 'note' but user requirement specifies 'reason' as the audit field name
findings.proposed_fix: Use 'reason' as the primary JSONL field; --note CLI param populates 'reason'
findings.rationale: User verbatim specifies {ts, agent, event, prev_score, new_score, delta, actor, reason}; JSONL must match

findings.issue: Schema over-expanded with mandatory event_id/seq/prev_event_id/source_command/batch fields not required by user
findings.proposed_fix: Keep event_id/seq/prev_event_id as optional (CAS-correctness enhancement per architect); remove source_command/batch as mandatory; make batch optional schema fields
findings.rationale: Mandatory ABA-prevention fields (event_id/seq/prev_event_id) retained because architect's critical finding is correct and low-cost; batch fields made optional; source_command removed as mandatory

findings.issue: --expected-prev-event-id is stronger than user requirement (CAS on prev_score)
findings.proposed_fix: Make --expected-prev-event-id optional; primary CAS comparison is on prev_score only
findings.rationale: User requirement says CAS on prev_score; event_id CAS is an enhancement for future cycles

findings.issue: Caller-side CAS under-specified: callers cannot safely read exact current score since score-inject.sh intentionally hides it
findings.proposed_fix: Make --expected-prev-score optional; normal callers use serialized-append mode (no CAS). CAS mode is available for explicit drift-detection callers.
findings.rationale: Normal callers (close.md, dev.md) must not break; --expected-prev-score optional preserves existing behavior

findings.issue: Derived agent-scores.json cache adds dual-state failure modes; user asked for lifecycle JSONL as source of truth
findings.proposed_fix: Remove derived cache. score-inject.sh reads lifecycle.jsonl directly. agent-scores.json write retained in score-update.sh for this cycle only for test backward-compatibility.
findings.rationale: Codex correctly identifies expansionist dual-state risk; lifecycle.jsonl is sole source of truth

findings.issue: Baseline-import idempotency unsafe: 'skip agents with existing baseline' fails if baseline appended after score events
findings.proposed_fix: Skip agents with ANY existing lifecycle entry (score_update or score_baseline_import), not just baseline entries
findings.rationale: Correct idempotency requires checking for any prior entry

findings.issue: Lifecycle tracking policy wording inaccurate: agent-scores.json is gitignored not tracked
findings.proposed_fix: State lifecycle.jsonl is intentionally tracked via !logs/lifecycle.jsonl exception; lifecycle.jsonl.lock is gitignored; add /logs/lifecycle.jsonl.lock to .gitignore
findings.rationale: Factual correction in spec wording and .gitignore scope

findings.issue: Malformed JSONL silent fallback hides audit corruption
findings.proposed_fix: score-inject.sh: neutral fallback ONLY for missing-file or no-entry-for-agent. Malformed JSONL exits 1 visibly.
findings.rationale: Correct failure mode distinction; prevents silent corruption

findings.issue: Recent-events display polluted by baseline_import entries
findings.proposed_fix: Filter last-3 recent events to kind:score_update only; baseline_import filtered from display
findings.rationale: User-facing injection text should show behavioral events, not migration artifacts
Correct organizational placement; CAS is a score-update/lifecycle contract

findings.issue: test_AC4 fix is test-maintenance not R9 functionality
findings.proposed_fix: Mark explicitly as stale generated-test maintenance; do not use to justify agent-scores.json cache retention
findings.rationale: Correct framing; test maintenance is required regardless of R9 but is not R9 scope

findings.issue: Append-only invariant needs concrete acceptance criteria
findings.proposed_fix: Added AC1-AC8 covering: append count, CAS exit 3, byte-preservation, score-inject reads lifecycle, baseline idempotency, test_AC4 pass, SKIP_FILES, gitignore
findings.rationale: ACs must be concrete and testable

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Round 1 (Codex session 019e5d9b): All 12 findings addressed. Round 2 (QA objections + Codex session 019e5dbc): OBJ-1: JSONL field 'note' renamed to 'reason' — M1 now explicitly requires renaming score-update.sh:171 field; AC1 verifies no 'note' in emitted JSON. OBJ-2: mandatory JSONL schema reduced to 8 R9-verbatim fields; event_id/seq/prev_event_id/kind marked advisory. OBJ-3: agent-scores.json write REMOVED from M1; contradicting constraint at ticket:288 and context:30 both fixed; agent-scores.json is now migration-input-only. OBJ-4: M5 eliminated (no-op); AC8 became AC9 (preflight precondition) with --error-unmatch flag added; new AC8 added for live baseline import verification (all 21 canonical agents). Additional Codex findings: Issue 1 (contradicting constraint) fixed; Issue 2 (CAS optional) acknowledged as intentional scope deviation documented in S1; Issue 3 (test_AC4 reads agent-scores.json history) — M8 expanded to full body rewrite targeting JSONL semantics; Issue 4 (malformed branch expectation) — M6 expanded with explicit malformed branch exit code 1 expectation; Issue 5 (AC8 non-discriminating) — fixed with --error-unmatch; Issue 6 (stale .gitignore in scope) — line 47 corrected; Issue 7 (hermetic test paths) — out_of_scope_observations (enhancement); Issue 8 (baseline import live execution) — new AC8 added; Issue 9 (kind wording in M2) — M2 and idempotency rule reworded to reference 'agent' field match, not kind. Round 3 (QA objections iteration 2): NEW-1: AC6 THEN clause field name corrected from 'uncapped_delta' to 'unclamped_score' (unclamped_score is the correct semantics per M8; uncapped_delta is the legacy score-update.sh emitted field name from before this cycle). NEW-2: M8 three-way contradiction resolved — only AC_UID and AC_TYPE constants are preserved verbatim; test body AND docstring must be rewritten entirely (the old docstring describes agent-scores.json semantics which are obsolete post-JSONL-rewrite); Technical Hints 'only the assertion values change' phrase removed. NEW-3: AC10 added to formally verify malformed JSONL causes exit code 1 with non-empty stderr and no INJECTION_PROOF in stdout — closes the gap where M6 prescribes the behavior but no AC enforced it. Round 4 (FINAL-1 + Codex session 019e5e18): FINAL-1 resolved: unclamped_score promoted from advisory to mandatory derived 9th lifecycle schema field. 8 R9-verbatim fields plus unclamped_score = prev_score + delta (pre-clamp arithmetic target; may exceed 100 or fall below 0). Schema header updated from '8 verbatim from R9' to '9 mandatory fields = 8 R9-verbatim + 1 derived clamp-audit field'. M1 now computes unclamped_score before clamping and emits it as mandatory field; does not emit legacy 'uncapped_delta'. M2 baseline entries use 9-field schema (unclamped_score = prev_score, delta=0). AC1 asserts all 9 fields including unclamped_score=prev_score+delta. AC6 GIVEN now requires the test to seed the temporary lifecycle JSONL with ba new_score=100 (so unclamped_score=101 is achievable). M8 rewrite instruction now explicitly seeds ba at new_score=100 before score-update.sh invocation. Advisory fields reduced to: kind, event_id, seq, prev_event_id.

score_injection_action: Operating at Senior Craftsman (61-80) with three consecutive close_fail_qa_pass(-5) events signals recent analytical failures — behavioral adjustment: applying maximum evidence-grounding rigor, citing every claim to a file:line, rejecting inferred root causes without measured support, and applying all 14 architect findings plus 12 Codex findings before finalizing scope.
